iti26:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti26:2.1:
“Monks, if beings knew, as I know, the results of giving & sharing,
they would not eat without having given, nor would the stain of selfishness overcome their minds.
iti26:2.2:
Even if it were their last bite, their last mouthful,
they would not eat without having shared, if there were someone to receive their gift.
iti26:2.3:
But because beings do not know, as I know, the results of giving & sharing, they eat without having given.
The stain of selfishness overcomes their minds.”
iti26:4.1: then, subduing the stain of selfishness
iti26:4.3: they’d give in season to the noble ones,
iti26:4.4: where a gift bears great fruit.
iti26:5.2: as an offering to those worthy of offerings,
iti26:5.3: many donors, when they pass away from here, the human state,
iti26:6.1: They, having gone there to heaven,
iti26:6.3: Unselfish, they partake of the result
